text:
Once upon a time, there was an old man named Oliver who lived in a quaint little cottage on top of a hill. Each morning, Oliver would wake up to the sound of his favorite old record—a jazz flute rendition of "Moonlight Sonata." Despite his age, he could often be found playing his recorder, humming jazz tunes, or strolling along the hillside near his garden.

One sunny morning, Oliver decided to build a birdhouse for the many birds he knew frequented his property. He took his time, gathering materials from his garden—some old branches, some twine, and a tiny piece of polished marble from a friend who had moved away. As he worked, he dreamt of the birds he might attract.

Finally, completed, Oliver placed it in a quiet corner of his garden. He named it "Lila." Little did he know, Lila had the heart of an adventure seeker. Every dawn, Lila would explore the horizon, chasing butterflies and singing old
